q: The ___ of the note (how high it is) is determined by its frequency.
The loudness of the note depends upon the ___ of the wave that produces it.
Greater amplitude leads to a louder note because more ___ is used to produce a larger amplitude.
___ or timbre is the characteristic which distinguishes two sounds of the same loudness and same pitch but emitted by different instruments.
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___, which varies with the number of overtones.
___ is a sensation and hence, unlike intensity, it depends on the observer.
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the ___ of the amplitude of the wave.
